ai.issueType: Modal trigger does not update aria-expanded when opening
ai.description: The modal open handler changes the modal display to block but does not set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button to indicate the dialog is open. Screen reader users are not informed that the controlled element is now visible.

ai.issueType: Modal close handler does not update aria-expanded on trigger
ai.description: The modal close handler hides the modal but does not set aria-expanded="false" on the original trigger button (.open-modal-btn). Screen reader users are not informed that the dialog has closed.

ai.issueType: Form validation errors not reflected in aria-invalid on submit
ai.description: When form validation fails on submit, error messages are displayed and input borders are colored red, but the aria-invalid attribute is not set to "true" on the invalid input fields. Screen readers will not announce the fields as invalid.

ai.issueType: aria-invalid not cleared when field becomes valid
ai.description: The blur event handler sets aria-invalid="true" when a field is empty, but never removes this attribute or sets it to "false" when the field contains valid input. Fields remain marked as invalid after users correct them.
The modal trigger button (.open-modal-btn) lacks an initial aria-expanded="false" attribute to indicate that the dialog is initially closed.
The menu button controls the visibility of the nav element but lacks the aria-controls attribute to establish the programmatic relationship between the trigger and the controlled navigation region.
The modal trigger button does not have an aria-controls attribute linking it to the modal element it controls, preventing screen readers from understanding the relationship.

ai.issueType: Load more content not announced to screen readers
ai.description: New items are appended to the DOM when the Load More button is clicked, but there is no aria-live announcement to inform screen reader users that new content has loaded or how many items were added.

ai.issueType: Cart count update not announced to screen readers
ai.description: When items are added to the cart, the counter text updates but screen reader users receive no notification that the cart has been updated or the new item count.

ai.issueType: Filter changes not announced to screen readers
ai.description: Filter selections update application state but do not announce the change to screen reader users, leaving them unaware that filter results may have updated.

ai.issueType: Form error messages not associated with inputs via aria-describedby
ai.description: Error message spans are created and appended to the DOM but are not programmatically associated with their corresponding input fields using aria-describedby. Screen readers cannot read the error message when the field is focused.

ai.issueType: Form validation creates duplicate error messages on resubmit
ai.description: Each time the form is submitted with invalid fields, new error message spans are appended without removing previous error messages, causing duplicate announcements and confusion for screen reader users.

ai.issueType: Modal lacks focus management when opening and closing
ai.description: When the modal opens, focus is not moved to the modal or its first focusable element. When closing, focus is not returned to the trigger button. Keyboard users lose their place in the page and may not know the modal has opened.

ai.issueType: Modal missing role and aria-modal attributes
ai.description: The modal element is shown by changing display to block, but the code does not set role="dialog" or aria-modal="true" on the modal container. Screen readers may not recognize it as a modal dialog that requires special handling.

ai.issueType: Form input blur handler does not clear aria-invalid
ai.description: The blur event listener sets aria-invalid="true" when a field is empty but never removes the attribute when the field becomes valid. This leaves the field permanently marked as invalid even after correction.

ai.issueType: Dynamically created error message not associated with input
ai.description: The error message span is created and appended to the DOM but is not linked to the input via aria-describedby. Users are not informed of the specific error message context.

ai.issueType: Modal trigger does not setup dialog accessibility
ai.description: Opening the modal sets display:block but fails to set role="dialog", aria-modal="true", or manage focus. Screen readers may not recognize it as a dialog, and focus may remain on the underlying page.

ai.issueType: Modal close button does not return focus
ai.description: Closing the modal hides it but does not return focus to the trigger button. Keyboard users are left without focus or must tab through the entire page to find their place.

lighthouse.issueType: Form elements do not have associated labels
lighthouse.description: Labels ensure that form controls are announced properly by assistive technologies, like screen readers. [Learn more about form element labels](https://dequeuniversity.com/rules/axe/4.11/label).

lighthouse.issueType: Form elements do not have associated labels
lighthouse.description: Labels ensure that form controls are announced properly by assistive technologies, like screen readers. [Learn more about form element labels](https://dequeuniversity.com/rules/axe/4.11/label).

lighthouse.issueType: Select elements do not have associated label elements.
lighthouse.description: Form elements without effective labels can create frustrating experiences for screen reader users. [Learn more about the `select` element](https://dequeuniversity.com/rules/axe/4.11/select-name).

lighthouse.issueType: Touch targets do not have sufficient size or spacing.
lighthouse.description: Touch targets with sufficient size and spacing help users who may have difficulty targeting small controls to activate the targets. [Learn more about touch targets](https://dequeuniversity.com/rules/axe/4.11/target-size).

lighthouse.issueType: Touch targets do not have sufficient size or spacing.
lighthouse.description: Touch targets with sufficient size and spacing help users who may have difficulty targeting small controls to activate the targets. [Learn more about touch targets](https://dequeuniversity.com/rules/axe/4.11/target-size).

lighthouse.description: Touch targets with sufficient size and spacing help users who may have difficulty targeting small controls to activate the targets. [Learn more about touch targets](https://dequeuniversity.com/rules/axe/4.11/target-size).
